demographic_audit_note: Verified Martha G. (77yo). Atlantis queue listed daughter's address in Newark as primary; cross-referenced with facility face sheet and confirmed Jersey City residence. Insurance verified as Horizon BCBS Advantage; noted potential zip code service gap for the preferred home health provider.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker (SW) confirms the goal is Home with specialized renal monitoring and dialysis support.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: SW indicates dialysis shunt site is healing well; patient is meeting PT goals for stair climbing. Target discharge window established for following Tuesday.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card saved to patient's smartphone contact list; program benefits flyer delivered to patient and daughter (POA) during bedside education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter 24 hours prior to discharge. Confirmed home is stocked with renal-safe meals and dialysis supplies. Daughter expressed anxiety regarding the gap between discharge and the first nurse visit.
atlantis_final_sync: Confirmed D/C date of 11/14 and scheduled 1st visit date of 11/15 entered into Atlantis. HHA SOC confirmed for morning window.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for 11/15 at 2:00 PM to ensure overlap with HHA nurse and verify medication reconciliation for renal meds.
narrative_summary: PN Martha G. initiated Stage 1 intake by auditing demographics and confirming a home discharge goal with the SW. During Stage 2, an Insurance-HHA Network Mismatch was discovered, necessitating a 4-day delay while the SW negotiated a single-case agreement for the patient's preferred agency. Stage 3 was completed successfully with a 24-hour pulse call to the daughter and the scheduling of the MA visit within 24 hours of the patient's return home.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis Audit: Address mismatch found between facility records and Atlantis queue.
Insurance-HHA Network Mismatch: Patient's Horizon plan does not initially cover the preferred HHA branch in Jersey City.
First Visit Coordination: HHA confirms single-case agreement reached; first visit window locked for Wednesday morning.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's insurance plan is out-of-network for the primary HHA in her zip code.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flagged the network mismatch to the SW and provided the HHA's contracting contact info for a single-case agreement.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter is confused about the timing of the first home nurse visit vs dialysis schedule.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Walked daughter through the Day 1 expectations, detailing when the nurse arrives and when the MA will follow up.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis records show an outdated phone number for the patient's daughter.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Audited the face sheet and updated the contact information in the Atlantis patient profile.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to finish the insurance negotiation for the out-of-network agency before initiating Stage 3 logistics.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW boundary regarding insurance authorizations and avoids premature scheduling that might be cancelled if the agreement fails.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Conduct the 24-hour pulse call to confirm home readiness and inform the SW that the family is anxious about the delay caused by the insurance mismatch.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Adheres to the 3-stage lifecycle by maintaining patient education and escalating barriers to the SW without overstepping into the negotiation itself.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the insurance company directly to advocate for the single-case agreement and suggest an alternative HHA that you know is in-network to speed up the discharge.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the Banned Actions by calling the insurance company for authorization and suggesting alternative HHA agencies to the SW.
